audit_title: SOC 2 Type II — Trust Services Criteria Compliance
framework: SOC 2 Type II (AICPA Trust Services Criteria 2017)
audit_scope: This audit evaluated the design and operating effectiveness of controls relevant to the Security, Availability, and Confidentiality trust services criteria for the Strata Cloud Platform operated by Strata Systems, Inc. The scope encompassed the production environment infrastructure, application security controls, access management, change management, incident response, vendor management, and data handling procedures across AWS us-east-1 and eu-west-1 regions.
standards_references:
1. AICPA Trust Services Criteria (2017) — Security (CC1–CC9), Availability (A1), Confidentiality (C1)
2. NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 (supplementary reference)
3. Strata Systems Information Security Policy v4.2
4. Strata Systems Change Management Procedure v3.1
exclusions:
1. Processing Integrity and Privacy criteria — not in scope per management assertion
2. Disaster recovery site in ap-southeast-1 — operational for less than 90 days
3. Strata Analytics add-on product — separate engagement scheduled for Q3 2026
overall_rating: Qualified — controls generally effective with exceptions noted
summary_opinion: In our opinion, the controls at Strata Systems, Inc. were suitably designed and operating effectively throughout the audit period to provide reasonable assurance that the trust services criteria for Security, Availability, and Confidentiality were met, with the exceptions noted in findings F-001 through F-009. The two High-severity findings relate to access review timeliness and encryption key rotation, both of which management has committed to remediating before the next audit period.

findings.title: Quarterly Access Reviews Not Completed Within Policy Timeframe
findings.description: Quarterly access reviews for privileged accounts were completed an average of 23 days late in Q4 2025. The Q3 2025 review for the database administration group was not completed at all.
findings.evidence: Access review completion records from IAM governance tool; interview with IAM team lead
findings.risk: Stale privileged access may persist beyond need, increasing the attack surface for credential compromise or insider threat
findings.recommendation: Implement automated access review workflows with escalation triggers at 7 and 14 days past due. Assign accountable owners for each review cycle.
findings.management_response: Accepted. Automated workflow implementation in progress — targeted completion by April 15, 2026.

findings.title: Encryption Key Rotation Exceeds 365-Day Policy Limit
findings.description: 14 of 38 customer data encryption keys in AWS KMS had not been rotated within the 365-day maximum defined in the Information Security Policy. The oldest key was 487 days since last rotation.
findings.evidence: AWS KMS key metadata export; policy document section 7.4.2
findings.risk: Extended key lifetimes increase the window for key compromise and reduce the effectiveness of encryption at rest controls
findings.recommendation: Enable automatic annual key rotation in AWS KMS for all customer data keys. Implement monitoring alerts for keys approaching 330 days without rotation.
findings.management_response: Accepted. Automatic rotation enabled for all keys as of February 12, 2026. Monitoring alert deployed.

findings.title: Vendor Risk Assessments Missing for Two Subprocessors
findings.description: Annual risk assessments were not completed for two subprocessors handling customer data: LogStream (log aggregation) and PagerRelay (incident notification). Both vendors were onboarded in 2024.
findings.risk: Unassessed third-party risk may expose customer data to controls gaps outside organizational oversight
findings.recommendation: Complete outstanding vendor assessments within 30 days. Integrate vendor review deadlines into the procurement calendar with automated reminders.
findings.management_response: Accepted. Assessments for both vendors scheduled for March 2026.

findings.title: Incident Response Tabletop Exercise Not Conducted in Audit Period
findings.description: The Information Security Policy requires semi-annual incident response tabletop exercises. Only one exercise was conducted during the 6-month audit period (October 2025). The second exercise, scheduled for January 2026, was postponed and not rescheduled.
findings.evidence: Incident response exercise log; team calendar records
findings.risk: Reduced preparedness for real incidents due to less frequent practice of response procedures
findings.recommendation: Schedule exercises at least 8 weeks apart with a designated backup date. Assign exercise coordination to a specific team member.
findings.management_response: Accepted. Exercise rescheduled for March 14, 2026. Backup dates now built into annual calendar.

findings.title: Change Management Records Incomplete for Emergency Changes
findings.description: 3 of 7 emergency changes deployed during the audit period did not have post-implementation reviews completed within the 5-business-day policy requirement. Retrospective documentation was added 12–18 days after deployment.
findings.evidence: Change management system records; deployment logs
findings.risk: Incomplete documentation of emergency changes reduces audit trail quality and may obscure introduced vulnerabilities
findings.recommendation: Add automated reminders for post-implementation review at 3 and 5 business days. Include PIR completion rate in weekly operations metrics.
findings.management_response: Accepted. Automated reminders configured in change management system.

positive_observations:
1. Zero critical findings for the second consecutive audit period
2. Multi-factor authentication enforced across 100% of user accounts, including service accounts accessing production
3. Mean time to detect security incidents improved from 34 minutes to 11 minutes compared to the prior audit
4. Infrastructure-as-code adoption at 96% with peer review required for all production changes
5. Employee security awareness training completion rate of 98% with phishing simulation click-through rate below 3%
recommendations_summary:
1. *Immediate (30 days):* Complete outstanding vendor risk assessments for LogStream and PagerRelay
2. *Short-term (60 days):* Deploy automated access review workflows with escalation policies
3. *Short-term (60 days):* Conduct rescheduled incident response tabletop exercise
4. *Ongoing:* Monitor encryption key rotation compliance via dashboard and alerts
5. *Ongoing:* Track emergency change PIR completion as an operational KPI
auditee_title: Chief Information Security Officer, Strata Systems, Inc.
